observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Establish a Disposal Committee to oversee and facilitate the proper disposal
of unserviceable properties of the MGPD;
2. Direct the MGSO to securely store unserviceable properties to prevent
further deterioration;
3. Require the Municipal Accountant to assist the MGSO in preparing the
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) by
providing the necessary information; and
4. Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2020-006 in order to establish PPE balances that are verifiable
as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Direct the MGSO, with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer’s Office
(MEO), to complete the Road Map with all necessary details;
2. Instruct the MAO to ensure that the LRNLCs contain complete and
accurate information;
3. Task the MGSO with completing the LRNPCs, ensuring all essential details
are included; and
4. Require the IC to thoroughly prepare the RPCLRN, addressing any gaps in
information to facilitate proper verification.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Secure and submit proof of an SPA, Extrajudicial Settlement, or Heir
Partition to establish the rightful authority of the sellers;
2. Provide the Sorsogon Provincial Appraisal/Valuation Report with the
complete signatures of all signatories to validate the property’s assessed
value;
3. Obtain the signed and dated acknowledgment from the four heirs, ensuring
their signatures appear on the left margin of all pages of the Deed of Waiver
of Rights and Quitclaim in compliance with notarization requirements;
4. Direct the Municipal Assessor’s Office to initiate the transfer of the property
title in the name of the MGPD to secure its legal ownership; and
5. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exercise due diligence in verifying
supporting documents before processing payments, in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Instruct the MGSO to regularly prepare and maintain the SPCs and the
RegSPI to ensure proper documentation and tracking of semi-expendable
properties, enhancing accountability and inventory management;
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper recording of semi-
expendable assets in the MGPD’s books of accounts and maintain the
SPLCs to effectively track and monitor these assets; and
3. Facilitate regular reconciliation between the MGSO and the MAO to align
records, minimize discrepancies, and enhance the accuracy of financial
reports.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to instruct the revenue
collectors to remit all succeeding collections regularly and intact as prescribed to
avoid accumulation and/or misappropriation.

We further recommended and Management agreed to continue the adherence
to this good practice and strong coordination between both offices.

We commended Management for its good practices on cash management,
particularly the remarkable efforts exerted by the concerned MAO personnel in
ensuring substantial settlement/liquidation of the CAs granted.

Also, we recommended and Management agreed, through the MAO, to
continue to comply strictly and consistently with the requirements of Section 89 of PD
No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 relative to the grant, utilization and
liquidation of CAs.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer
to promptly document all details of the DMs and CMs processed by the depository
bank immediately upon receipt of the BS to facilitate the timely reconciliation of
differences.

Additionally, we recommended and Management agreed to require the
Municipal Accountant to address all reconciling items identified in the BRS to ensure
accurate reporting of the account balance in the FS.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prioritize
the reconciliation of its records with the DA to facilitate the necessary
adjustments in the books of accounts and in the cashbook, respectively, and
to correct the abnormal balance of the Due to NGAs account;
2. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to
reclassify the Due to NGAs account into the appropriate accounts in
accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to come
up with fairly presented FS;
3. Direct the MAO to exert utmost effort in retrieving the necessary documents
to support the accuracy and validity of the balances recorded under the Due
to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts, and thereafter, request for the write-
off of dormant unliquidated fund transfers in accordance with COA Circular
No. 2023-008, if warranted;
4. Ensure proper accounting of the utilization of fund transfers received
through accurate recording of transactions and proper maintenance of SLs
and supporting schedules;
5. Submit the FURs promptly to SAs in accordance with the terms and
conditions stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) or the
provisions under COA Circular No. 94-013, in case no reportorial
requirements are provided in the MOA; and
6. Conduct periodic reconciliation of its records with the SAs and the PGS to
arrive at correct/reconciled balances of the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs
accounts, respectively.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:
1. Observe strictly the proper recognition of liabilities, only when goods are
already delivered or services are rendered, pursuant to the above-cited
regulations;
2. Ensure that supporting documents are attached when recording accounts
payable to validate the legitimacy of the claim;
3. Verify the long outstanding obligations, and if found to have no valid or
legal claims against the MGPD, cause the reversion of the same to the
unappropriated surplus of the GF; and
4. Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 98 of PD No. 1445 regarding the
reversion of long outstanding payables.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Thoroughly evaluate the proposed priority development PPAs under the
20% DF to ensure their readiness for implementation or procurement,
thereby preventing delays or non-implementation;
2. Ensure the timely execution of all PPAs to maximize the efficient use of
public funds and delivery of the intended benefits to the community; and
3. Conduct an extensive review of the unimplemented PPAs to determine
whether these projects are still feasibly implementable and reprogram those
that are considered non-priority PPAs.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Identify the specific DRRM-related PPAs to be funded by annual and
supplemental budgets prior to submission of the same to the SB for approval
in order to minimize the processing time, fast track the utilization of the
fund and avoid disallowances in audit;
2. Direct the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with Items
5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in order to come up with a
complete and comprehensive plan that will serve as basis in the
implementation of the MGPD’ DRRM-related PPAs;
3. Enjoin the MDRRMO to exert more effort in implementing actions and
measures pertaining to all aspects of DRRM towards building a disaster-
resilient LGU and communities; and
4. Direct the concerned offices to prepare and maintain complete and updated
records on LDRRMF and to monitor closely the utilization and
implementation of all PPAs under the said fund to facilitate the timely
reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum utilization.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate transfer of
unspent DRRMF from the GF to the STF and thereafter, require the
Municipal Accountant to properly take up the transfer in the books of the
Municipality;
2. Require the MAO, the Municipal Budget Office (MBO) and the MDRRMO
to coordinate with each other in order to come up with accurate and reliable
reports on LDRRMF; and
3. Require the MDRRMO and the MAO to prepare and submit the Report on
Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis to the AT within the
prescribed period.

Likewise, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
1. Maintain SLs for the unutilized DRRMF in accordance with Section 5.1.12
of COA Circular No. 2012.002; and
2. Reclassify the amount of ₱3,888,000.00 from the Motor Vehicles account to
the Construction and Heavy Equipment account (₱2,090,000.00) and the
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account (₱1,798,000.00).

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the MHRMO to:
1. Prioritize the formulation of a “Qualification Standards and Salary Rates
for JO Positions” and to come up with an HRP to be submitted to the SP for
subsequent enactment of a resolution adopting the same;
2. Issue a uniform policy guideline on the hiring of JO personnel to properly
determine the necessity of hiring JO and to strengthen internal controls
pertaining thereto; and
3. Properly prepare JOs for the hiring of necessary JO personnel and indicate
therein the specific duties, functions and deliverables expected from them as
basis in evaluating their accomplishment reports and the propriety of
payments to them, pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations.

Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to observe the provisions
of Section 2, Rule XI of CSC MC No. 40, series of 1998 and Section 211(b) of the
GAAM, Volume I, in resorting to hiring of casual or emergency staff to avoid hiring a
significant number of JO personnel.

Further, we recommended and the Committee on Rules of the SB agreed to take
appropriate action to address all matters pertaining to the organization and
management; personnel administration, position classification and pay plan, staffing
patterns; and creation of positions and policy formulation, in connection with the
observations noted in audit.

Nonetheless, we recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Follow strictly the prescribed timeline in the preparation of the annual SEF
Budget as provided in DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017;
2. Require the LSB to prepare a three-year program indicating strategic
prioritization policies in the allocation of the SEF to schools, prior to the
formulation and preparation of its Budget and furnish the AT for reference
and review purposes;
3. Support the SEF budget with plan of intended priority PPAs to be funded
out of the fund presented by allotment class and per school;
4. Require the Municipal Treasurer, the LSB and the MBO to coordinate
properly so that accurate figures are included in the SEF Budget
computation; and
5. Discontinue the practice of charging expenditures to the SEF that are not
aligned with its primary purpose, and strictly adhere to the regulations
governing prioritization of expenses chargeable to the SEF.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor closely the preparation of
needed schedules for remittance of withheld taxes and ensure remittance in
full within the prescribed deadlines; and
2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amounts
withheld to the BIR and ensure that monthly payments correspond to the
exact amounts withheld to avoid penalties and surcharges.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Remit to the GSIS all outstanding balance due to the said agency; and
2. Comply continuously with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper
deduction and remittance of GSIS contributions in accordance with Section
6(b) of RA No. 8291.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Require the MHRMO to submit to the MAO the lacking Pag-IBIG numbers
of the JO personnel with unremitted contributions. Otherwise, require the
JO employees to apply for Pag-IBIG Membership Identification and inform
immediately the MAO for remittance purposes;
2. Return to the JO personnel the previous years’ deducted Pag-IBIG
contributions; and
3. Comply religiously with existing laws, rules and regulations on deductions
and remittance of HDMF contributions in accordance with the provisions of
RA No. 9679.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Require the MHRMO to submit to the MAO the lacking PhilHealth
numbers of personnel with unremitted contributions;
2. Return to the concerned personnel the previous years’ deducted PhilHealth
contributions; and
3. Comply continuously with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper
deduction and remittance of PhilHealth contributions in accordance with
Section 18 of the Revised IRR of National Health Insurance Act of 2013 or
RA No. 7875, as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the GFPS to strengthen
the monitoring of implementation of the annual GPB and establish a GAD
Monitoring and Evaluation system to optimize GAD Fund utilization and PPA
implementation.

We reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to set
clear goals for the GAD database’s development and regularly monitor its
progress and gather feedback for improvement to ensure that the database
meets the Municipality’s needs effectively.

In addition, we recommended and Management agreed to require the GFPS to
conduct gender analysis to substantiate indicated gender issues and PPAs in the GAD
GPB and utilize HGDG, PIMME/FIMME tools to identify, analyze and monitor its
responsiveness and effectiveness.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to donate the various existing buildings and health centers being used by the Barangays in their localities, and facilitate immediately the subsequent transfer thereof.  Thereafter, submit the relevant documents to the MAO to drop the subject properties from the books in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MAO to prepare immediately the necessary correcting entry to reclassify the properties amounting to ₱742,537.50 with acquisition costs below ₱50,000.00 as Inventories if not yet issued, otherwise as appropriate Expenses if already issued.  Likewise, ensure that the recoding of PPE account is in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2022-004.

Nevertheless, we recommended and the Management agreed to:
Continue to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of the MGPD’s funds while limiting expenditures within the total income realized and collected during the year to avoid cash deficiency; and
Invest the idle cash in major infrastructure projects or the establishment of economic enterprise or other PPAs that can help improve the livelihood and general welfare of the MGPD’s constituents.  However, relative to audit observation no. 5 on the discrepancy of records between the MAO and the Municipal Treasury Office (MTO), the said Offices shall reconcile first their records to arrive at the correct amount of cash savings that will be available for appropriation.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MA and the MT to conduct regular reconciliation of the MGPD’s cash accounts to come up with the correct balances thereof.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MAO to prepare the BRS on regular basis and on time by requesting from the depository bank a snapshot of the BS if such cannot be furnished to Management at the end of each month.  Henceforth, submit the BRS to the AT within 10 days after the end of each month pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 for proper verification.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to, through the MT, require the revenue collectors to:
Remit promptly their collections in accordance with the prescribed period of remittance;
Prepare always the RCD every time they remit their collections to show the actual date of remittance to the MTO; and
Prepare individual cashbooks to monitor properly their collections and accountable forms.
We also recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MT to monitor and enforce strict remittance of collections by revenue collectors to avoid the accumulation of cash and possible loss thereof through theft or misappropriation.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Require the MA to ensure proper accounting of the utilization of fund transfers received from NGAs through accurate recording of transactions and proper maintenance of SLs and supporting schedules;
Follow up the release of fund from the DA that was intended for the Provision of Support Facilities to Farmers/Fisheries; and
Revisit the non-moving unexpended balances of fund transfers received from various NGAs and thereafter, return the balances to the SAs if properly identifiable or make representations and obtain authority to utilize the unexpended balances for purposes other than what are provided in the Memorandum of Agreement.

We commended Management’s efforts in ensuring prompt implementation of completed PPAs indicating efficient and timely utilization of fund transfers received from various agencies intended for the welfare of its constituents.
Nevertheless, we recommended and Management agreed to plan meticulously the implementation phase of PPAs in order to optimally utilize the grants received from the SAs within the agreed timeframe and the benefits attached thereto can be immediately enjoyed by the recipients.

We commended Management for carrying out measures for the timely liquidation of the CAs granted to officials and employees.
Likewise, we recommended and LCE agreed to:
Continue to comply strictly with the requirements of Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-001 and all other related rules and regulations pertaining to the grant, utilization and liquidation of CA; and
Continue the good practice of monitoring the CAs granted to officials and employees to ensure timely liquidation thereof.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MA to determine the validity of the long outstanding obligations, and if found to have no valid or legal claims against the MGPD, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus.
Likewise, we recommended and the MA agreed that the Adjusting Entry item amounting to ₱186,198.50 must be fully examined in order to determine its nature and effect the necessary adjustments in the books, if warranted.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MA to:
Determine the journal entries made to record the balance of the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account under the GF and effect the necessary adjusting entries; and
Transfer the unutilized LDRRMF-QRF and MF-MOOE as of December 31, 2022 to the STF.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MDRRMO to conduct adequate and proper planning in determining PPAs to be allocated with the LDRRMF. Accordingly, monitor closely the implementation of the said PPAs to facilitate corrective action, if any, and the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for optimum utilization.
We also recommended that the MDRRMO review the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) for the DRRMF received from other sources, particularly those allotted for typhoons but not utilized, otherwise, coordinate with the SAs to determine the necessary actions for the utilization/disposition of the said funds.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineer (ME) to plan properly and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave way for timely implementation and completion;
Require the Local Development Council, Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC) and the ME to monitor constantly the status of project implementation to introduce necessary interventions at the earliest time possible; and
Direct the MPDC and ME to conduct intensive review of continuing projects funded under the 20% DF to determine the PPAs that are still feasibly implementable and reprogram projects that are not needed to other priority PPAs.

We commended Management for its notable accomplishment in the timely submission of the CY 2022 annual FS to the Office of the Auditor.
Likewise, we recommended and LCE agreed to require the MAO to comply strictly with the submission of the required financial reports, together with all the supporting schedules, on or before the prescribed deadline pursuant to Sections 70 and 73 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended and LCE agreed to require the:
Implementing units to closely coordinate with the MPDC and assess whether there is still a need to appropriate funds for COVID-19 related expenses under the 20% DF.  Otherwise, use the budget to finance the other worthwhile development projects of the LGU; and
Concerned offices to maximize the utilization of their COVID-19 funds in order to ensure timely delivery of adequate and appropriate services to more constituents who are greatly affected by the pandemic.

We recommended and LCE agreed to require the RHU to:
Submit the PTRs and duly accomplished supporting documents to the MT and MAO to facilitate the evaluation of the receipt and issuances/utilization of COVID-19 vaccines, ancillary supplies and rice incentives and the recording of the remaining balances of unused inventories in the books and property records;
Coordinate with the PGS to secure the documents that contain the costs of the items donated as needed for the recording thereof; and
Ensure to align the inventory procedures with existing rules and regulations.
We recommended and Management agreed to require the MHO and RHU to plan properly the utilization and distribution of COVID-19 vaccines, especially those nearly expiring to prevent wastage thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue with their strategies of encouraging residents to be vaccinated in their present and future campaigns in battling COVID-19 or other viruses for their own protection and the public at large.

We recommended and LCE agreed to require the:
MT to prepare the certified list of RPT and SET due and collectible based on the RPTARs or TICs and submit to the MA at the beginning of each year to set up the receivables; and
MA to set up the receivable based on the certified list submitted by the MTO.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit immediately the DTRs, ARs and other necessary documents to support payment of wages to casuals/JO personnel amounting to ₱9,425,428.84 lest these will be suspended in audit.
Henceforth, we recommended and the LCE agreed to direct:
The department heads, as immediate supervisors of the casuals/JO personnel concerned, to require the latter to prepare the monthly ARs prior to the approval of their respective DTRs; and
The HRMO to compile and check the completeness of the DTRs and ARs of all casuals/JO personnel and submit the same to the MAO for attachment to DVs.

We commended Management’s efforts in ensuring the gender-responsiveness, correctness and alignment of the entries contained in its GPB by obtaining the certificate of review and endorsement from the DILG Sorsogon Provincial Office.
On the other hand, we recommended and the GFPS agreed to:
Ensure timely submission of GAD-related reports to the AT in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2014-001; and
Maintain compliance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 on the preparation and submission of GAD-related reports.

Nevertheless, we reiterated our recommendations and the GFPS agreed to:
Establish a complete GAD database by gathering other sex-disaggregated data, placing priority on the data relevant to the GAD issues indicated in the GPB to ensure that the said issues represent actual gender gaps and/or differences among the constituents of the Municipality; and
Allot the necessary budget in in setting-up and maintaining the GAD database.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to:
Prepare and submit to the AT the schedule of remittances of taxes withheld chargeable to SEF, MDF, and TF;
Effect the necessary adjusting entries to record accurately the withholding and remittance of taxes for CY 2022; and
Record subsequent withholding and remittance of taxes in accordance with the accounting procedures required under COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Provide regularly the GSIS with report/information on changes in the employee records, salary or status of employment, if any;
Remit to the GSIS all of the outstanding balance due to the said agency; and
Comply continuously with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deduction and remittance of GSIS contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of RA No. 8291.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the MHRMO to submit to the MAO the lacking PhilHealth numbers of personnel with unremitted contributions;
Return to the concerned personnel the previous years’ deducted PhilHealth contributions; and
Comply continuously with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deduction and remittance of PhilHealth contributions in accordance with Section 18 of the Revised IRR of National Health Insurance Act of 2013 or RA No. 7875 as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to seek authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) for the Municipal Mayor to donate the various PPE being used by other government agencies which are still existing, and immediately facilitate the subsequent transfer thereof to these agencies.  Thereafter, submit the relevant documents to the MAO for the dropping of the subject properties from the books in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386.

We commended Management for duly incorporating all COVID-19-related PPAs in its AIP, LDRRMFIP, supplemental AIPs and MOA in compliance with rules and regulations governing COVID-19 fund receipts and utilization.  Moreover, we recommended and Management agreed to continue to comply with the rules and regulations pertaining to the disbursement of COVID-19-related PPAs.

We commended the efforts extended by Management for the timely implementation of PPAs funded by various NGAs that resulted in attaining satisfactorily the purpose of the fund transfers.
We also recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
Concerned Offices to fast track the implementation of PPAs funded by the NGAs so that benefits attached thereto can be enjoyed by the recipients as soon as possible;
MA and the MT to return the unutilized fund balances of completed PPAs to concerned SAs; and
Comply strictly with the requirements of MOA and/or general guidelines on the utilization and reporting of fund transferred by various NGAs.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the MA to confirm the balances of all the Due to NGAs accounts and/or make representation to the concerned SAs to reconcile their respective records.
Otherwise, Management shall return the said unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury, if the documents or records of the specific accounts cannot be located or trace anymore.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineer to plan properly and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave way for timely implementation and completion;
Require the Local Development Council, MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to monitor constantly the status of project implementation to introduce necessary interventions at the earliest time possible; and
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Council and Municipal Engineer to conduct intensive review of continuing projects funded under the 20% DF to determine the PPAs that are still feasibly implementable and reprogram projects that are not needed to other priority PPAs.

We recommended that Management ensure that the identified priority PPAs are implemented as planned and to utilize fully the LDRRMF within the scheduled timeframes.  In such manner, the in-house capability of the MGPD to efficiently manage all types of emergencies and to achieve orderly transitions from response to sustained recovery in case of calamity will be strengthened.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO and MAO to:
Observe constant reconciliation of records and communicate immediately any adjustment to be taken up in the agency’s records to correct the financial reports; and
Revise or effect the necessary adjustments in their respective reports to present fairly the agency’s records.

We recommended that Management require all MDRRMO personnel to attend the necessary mandatory trainings that will boost their capabilities to counter the effects and mitigate the impact of calamities and also help them in the efficient and effective management of DRRM-related projects and activities.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to:
Establish the GAD database by gathering sex-disaggregated data, placing priority on the data relevant to the GAD issues indicated in the GPB to ensure that the said issues represent actual gender gaps and/or differences among the constituents of the Municipality; and
Allot the necessary budget in setting-up and maintaining the GAD database.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the MA to:
Reconcile the amounts withheld or deducted with the amounts remitted to determine the actual amounts of Due to BIR.  Thereafter, prepare and record the necessary adjusting entry, if any; and
Coordinate with the previous MA to turn-over pertinent records particularly the details of the prior year transactions and prepare subsidiary ledgers (SL), if warranted.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) to submit to the MAO the lacking HDMF numbers of the job order personnel with unremitted contributions.  Otherwise, require the job order employees to apply for Pag-IBIG MID and immediately inform the accounting office for remittance purposes;
Require the MA to investigate the negative balances noted in the Due to Pag-IBIG account and effect the necessary adjusting entries, if any; and

Resolve the legal issue concerning the current practice and the protocol of enforcing civil remedies vis a vis Section 247 of the same Code; and

Prepare an individual list of delinquent properties showing the number of years of delinquency to have an overview of properties that are possible candidate for remedies.
observations.reason: Limited manpower to prepare a full list of delinquent taxpayers. This recommendation is reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant with duly certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible at the beginning of the year in order to present a reliable and/or correct RPT/SET Receivables Accounts. Once submitted, direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting/adjusting entries based on the certified list to reflect the correct RPT/SET Receivables. With the actual collectibles established, collection efforts may be geared up to increase funding source of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Limited manpower to prepare a full list of delinquent taxpayers. This recommendation is reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the pertinent rules and regulations on the inventory and control of PPE by requiring the General Services Officer (GSO) or a designated personnel and the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of the Municipality’s PPE and, thereafter, prepare an inventory report.
observations.managementAction: The GSO started to update the existing PPE records in preparation for the conduct of physical count.
observations.reason: Management was unable to create an Inventory Committee due to lack of manpower. Likewise, the GSO had not been well-oriented with the step by step procedures in the conduct of the physical count of the PPE.

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management designate a qualified LGU personnel in the LDRRMO in order to organize and establish the LDRRMO in accordance with the provisions set forth in RA No. 10121 and National Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (NDRRMC)-DILG- Department of Budget and Management (DBM)-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014 - 01 dated April 4, 2014 so that efforts will be coordinated in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of disaster risk reduction and management towards building disaster-resilient local communities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the pertinent rules and regulations on the inventory and control of PPE and create an Inventory Committee who, together with the General Service Officer (GSO) shall conduct physical count of the Municipality’s PPE and thereafter, prepare and submit the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the requirements of IPSAS 17 and make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected asset and equity accounts.
Also, we recommended that the following adjusting journal entries be made to take up the unrecognized depreciation.

We recommended that Management require the:
Designated GSO and the Municipal Accountant to have proper coordination regarding the issue on the inventory accounts so that the procurement and issuance of the same would be properly recorded and presented in the financial statements; and
Designated GSO to submit the SSMI of the issued inventories to the Municipal Accountant in order for the latter to draw the JEV for the dropping of the subject inventories.

We recommended that the LCE:
Direct all the concerned accountable officials and employees to liquidate/settle their cash advances immediately;
Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the concerned accountable officials/employees to liquidate/settle immediately their cash advances.  Copy of the said demand letters bearing the receipts of the concerned accountable officials/employees should be furnished the Audit Team; and
Adhere strictly to the provision of Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 of not processing Disbursement Vouchers for additional cash advances of Municipal officials and employees with outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that Management:
Require the BAC, the Secretariat and the TWG to strictly abide by the standards set forth by RA No. 9184, its Revised IRR and other relevant laws on government procurement;
Require them also to exercise due diligence in the performance of their duties to ensure that all requirements of the procurement law are faithfully adhered to; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to observe the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 at all times.

We recommended that Management require the GSO and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain the required property records to effectively identify and monitor all the PPE of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Create a disposal committee who will conduct a complete physical count of all the unserviceable properties; and
Prepare the IIRUP and facilitate the disposal of the said properties in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386 on the disposal of unserviceable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace and analyze the pertinent transactions, and reconcile the noted difference in the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the said accounts.

We recommended that Management prioritize the funding for the update of the CLUP. Subsequently, outsource/hire a personnel with technical expertise related to the updating of the said plan so that the Municipality can update the said plan.
